summary: Fix race in AdaptiveLimiter.Wait() by making lastRequest a monotone 'next available slot' pointer rather than a call-time snapshot. Each caller claims a distinct delay-spaced slot under the lock and targets that slot for its sleep.
reason: Greptile P2 finding on PR #444 (ratelimit.go:43-57) + P1 follow-up (ratelimit.go:68). The first fix held the lock across the read-decide-update sequence but still set lastRequest=time.Now() *before* sleeping; two callers 1ms apart could compute the same wake-time and burst, defeating the rate limiter at exactly the same edge the original TOCTOU was meant to close. The monotone-slot shape is race-free because the slot is committed under the lock; later callers see the advanced lastRequest and compute a strictly later slot.
validated_outcome: go test ./internal/cliutil/... PASS; build/vet clean. Manual trace: G1 at T=0 advances lastRequest to delay; G2 at T=1ms reads lastRequest=delay, computes next=2*delay, slots are now strictly disjoint.
